paper_title: Mid-Holocene climate change in Europe: a data-model comparison

methods.description: The overall process for comparing climate change patterns derived from proxy data with those simulated by climate models. It involves identifying homogenous climate change regions in proxy data, assigning model grid boxes to the most similar data clusters, and evaluating the model-data fit using distance measures and comparison statistics.

steps.description: Identify regions of homogenous climate changes in the proxy data using cluster analysis, giving a set of "data clusters".
steps.searchable_summary: Perform cluster analysis on proxy climate data to identify homogenous regions.
inputs: Proxy climate data (bioclimatic variables, geographical coordinates)
outputs: Data clusters (centroids, extent, assigned data points)

steps.description: On the basis of the simulated changes in the same parameters, each model grid box is assigned to the most similar data cluster.
steps.searchable_summary: Assign each climate model grid box to the most similar proxy data cluster.
outputs: Model grid box assignments (mapping each grid box to a data cluster)

steps.description: The similarity is assessed by measuring the distance in climate space between the data cluster and the model grid box. This distance is measured using a fuzzy distance measure, which takes into account the uncertainties on both the data and the model.
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate fuzzy distance between model grid box simulated changes and data cluster centroids.
inputs: Climate model output (simulated climate changes for a grid box)

steps.description: In addition, by accumulating the distances between the model and the data, a general estimate of the "fit" between data and model may be obtained.
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate overall model-data fit based on accumulated distances.
inputs: Fuzzy distances for all model grid boxes and their assigned clusters

steps.description: Once the gridboxes of a model have been assigned to the clusters, a set of comparison statistics are calculated.
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate comparison statistics between model output and data clusters.
outputs: Comparison statistics (median climate distance, median geographical distance, number of clusters found, magnitude ratio)

methods.description: Procedure to group proxy data points showing similar sign and amplitude of climate change using the K-means algorithm. Clusters represent regions with internally similar climate change, incorporating both climate variables and geographical location for spatial coherence.

steps.description: The clusters are defined on the available bioclimatic variables (GDD5, MTCO, AET/PET) and the geographical coordinates of each grid point.
steps.searchable_summary: Prepare input data matrix including bioclimatic variables and geographical coordinates for each proxy data point.
outputs: Input data matrix for clustering (N samples x 5 features)

steps.description: The k-means algorithm was used for cluster analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: Apply K-means clustering algorithm to the input data matrix.
outputs: Cluster assignments for each data point

steps.description: In order to select an optimum number [of groups], we use the ratio of inter to intra-group variance of an increasing number of groups, and consider a stopping rule when the gain was less than 0.05, resulting in the selection of five groups.
steps.searchable_summary: Determine optimal number of clusters (k) using variance ratio and a stopping rule.

steps.description: Each group can be characterised by its centroid or centre of gravity, the average point in the multidimensional space defined by the variables.
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate the centroid for each identified cluster.

steps.description: We then test to see if a different set of groups may be obtained if a different random start is used by examining a) if other possible sets of centroids may be obtained; and b) the amount by which the centroids of these groups vary. The k-means algorithm was run 1000 times using a different random start each time.
steps.searchable_summary: Test cluster stability by running K-means multiple times with different random initializations.
outputs: Multiple sets of cluster centroids (1000 sets)

steps.description: and the standard deviation of the value attributed to the centroid was calculated.
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate the standard deviation of centroid values across multiple runs.
outputs: Standard deviation of centroid values for each dimension

steps.description: A low value means that the centroids do not vary significantly, and the set of five clusters obtained can be considered as stable.
steps.searchable_summary: Assess cluster stability based on the standard deviation of centroid values.

methods.description: Procedure for comparing climate model output to the identified proxy data clusters. This involves assigning each model grid box to the most similar data cluster using a fuzzy distance measure that accounts for uncertainties and calculating various statistics to evaluate the model's performance.

steps.description: The similarity is assessed by measuring the distance in climate space between the data cluster and the model grid box. This distance is measured using a fuzzy distance measure, which takes into account the uncertainties on both the data and the model.
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate fuzzy distance between simulated climate changes at a model grid box and the centroid of a data cluster, considering uncertainties.
inputs: Simulated climate changes for a model grid box (vector)
outputs: Fuzzy distance value for the grid box and cluster

steps.description: On the basis of the simulated changes in the same parameters, each model grid box is assigned to the most similar data cluster.
steps.searchable_summary: Assign each model grid box to the data cluster with the minimum fuzzy distance.
outputs: Assigned data cluster ID for each model grid box

steps.description: Calculate the median climate distance between the data clusters and the model. This measure allows an assessment of how well the model reproduces the value of the reconstructed changes, and is location independent.
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate the median fuzzy distance between each model grid box and its assigned data cluster.
inputs: Fuzzy distances for all model grid boxes and their assigned clusters

steps.description: Calculate the median geographical distance between the location of each model grid box and its associated data cluster. This allows an assessment of how closely situated each model grid box is to its associated data cluster.
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate the median geographical distance between model grid box locations and the geographical centroids of their assigned data clusters.
inputs: Assigned data cluster ID for each grid box

steps.description: Calculate the number of clusters found in the model. This is used to assess whether the model simulates the same types of changes found in the reconstruction.
steps.searchable_summary: Count the number of unique data clusters assigned to model grid boxes.
inputs: Assigned data cluster ID for each model grid box
outputs: Number of unique clusters represented in the model output

steps.description: Calculate the ratio between the magnitude of reconstructed and simulated climate changes.
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate the ratio of the magnitude of climate changes between the original proxy data and the simulated changes in the model output.
